`);\n\n  return db;\n}\n\n/**\n * Create an in-memory database with push subscriptions schema.\n */\nexport function createPushDatabase(): Database.Database {\n  const db = new `);\n\n  // Push subscriptions schema with owner_id for authorization\n  db.exec(`\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS push_subscriptions (\n      endpoint_hash TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n      endpoint TEXT NOT ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Create a test trip record with default values.\n */\nexport function createTestTrip(\n  overrides: Partial<{\n    id: string;\n    date: string;\n    originId: string;\n    originName: string;\n    destinationId: string;\n    destinationName: string;\n    line: string;\n    departureTime: number;\n    arrivalTime: number;\n    actualDurationMinutes: number;\n    scheduledDurationMinutes: number;\n    source: \"manual\" | \"inferred\" | \"tracked\";\n    notes: string;\n  }> = {}\n) {\n  const now = overrides.arrivalTime ?? now,\n    actualDurationMinutes: overrides.actualDurationMinutes ?? 60,\n    scheduledDurationMinutes: overrides.scheduledDurationMinutes ?? 55,\n    source: (overrides.source ?? \"manual\") as \"manual\" | \"inferred\" | \"tracked\",\n    notes: overrides.notes,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a test push subscription.\n */\nexport function createTestSubscription(\n  overrides: overrides.endpoint ?? \"https://fcm.googleapis.com/fcm/send/test-endpoint\",\n      keys: {\n        p256dh: overrides.p256dh ?? \"test-p256dh-key\",\n        auth: overrides.auth ?? \"test-auth-key\",\n      },\n    },\n    favorites: overrides.favorites ?? [\n      { id: \"fav-1\", stationId: \"101\", lines: [\"1\"], direction: \"both\" },\n    ],\n    quietHours: overrides.quietHours ?? { enabled: false, startHour: 22, endHour: 7 },\n    morningScores: overrides.morningScores ?? {},\n  };\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Authentication helpers for API tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport interface TestAuthCredentials {\n  keyId: string;\n  apiKey: string;\n  authorizationHeader: string;\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a test API key with the specified scope and role.\n * Returns the credentials needed to make authenticated requests.\n */\nexport async function createTestApiKey(\n  scope: \"read\" | \"write\" | \"admin\" = \"write\",\n  role: \"guest\" | \"user\" | \"admin\" = \"user\"\n): Promise<TestAuthCredentials> {\n  const keyId = `test_key_${Math.random().toString(36).substring(7)}`;\n  const apiKey = await generateApiKey();\n  const hashed = await hashApiKey(apiKey);\n\n  const keyData = {\n    keyId,\n    keyHash: hashed.hash,\n    keySalt: hashed.salt,\n    scope,\n    rateLimitTier: 10,\n    active: true,\n    createdAt: Date.now(),\n    expiresAt: 0,\n    role,\n  };\n\n  await registerApiKey(keyData);\n  registerApiKeyWithMetadata(keyData, `Test ${role} key with ${scope} scope`);\n\n  return {\n    keyId,\n    apiKey,\n    authorizationHeader: `Bearer ${keyId}:${apiKey}`,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Create test credentials for an admin user.\n */\nexport async function createTestAdminCredentials(): Promise<TestAuthCredentials> {\n  return createTestApiKey(\"admin\", \"admin\");\n}\n\n/**\n * Create test credentials for a regular user with write access.\n */\nexport async function createTestUserCredentials(): Promise<TestAuthCredentials> {\n  return createTestApiKey(\"write\", \"user\");\n}\n\n/**\n * Create test credentials for a read-only user.\n */\nexport async function createTestReadCredentials(): Promise<TestAuthCredentials> {\n  return createTestApiKey(\"read\", \"user\");\n}\n\n/**\n * Clear all commute stats cache (for test isolation).\n */\nexport function clearCommuteStatsCache(db: Database.Database): void {\n  db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM commute_stats\").run();\n}\n\n/**\n * Clear all trips (for test isolation).\n */\nexport function clearAllTrips(db: Database.Database): void {\n  db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM trips\").run();\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Module state reset helpers (for test isolation)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Reset all module-level state between tests.\n * This function resets cache, trip tracking, and alerts state\n * to prevent test pollution from module-level singletons.\n *\n * @deprecated Use {@link cleanupAllState} instead — it covers every known\n * module-level singleton.\n */\nexport async function resetAllModuleState(): Promise<void> {\n  await cleanupAllState();\n}\n\n/**\n * Comprehensive cleanup of ALL shared mutable state across the server package.\n *\n * Call this in a `beforeEach` hook (or at the start of any test that needs\n * isolation) so that no module-level Map / Set / array leaks from a\n * previous test into the current one.\n *\n * Each step is guarded so that individual test files which `vi.mock()` a\n * module (replacing its exports with stubs) will not cause the entire\n * cleanup to blow up — the missing reset function is simply skipped.\n */\nexport async function cleanupAllState(): Promise<void> {\n  // Helper: call `fn` only if it exists on the imported module.\n  const tryReset = async (spec: string, ...fns: string[]): Promise<void> => {\n    try {\n      const mod = await import(spec);\n      for (const fn of fns) {\n        if (typeof mod[fn] === \"function\") {\n          await mod[fn]();\n        }\n      }\n    } catch {\n      // Module may be mocked or not reachable — skip silently.\n    }\n  };\n\n  // ---- cache.ts ----------------------------------------------------------\n  await tryReset(\"../cache.js\", \"resetAllCacheStateForTesting\");\n\n  // ---- alerts-poller.ts ---------------------------------------------------\n  await tryReset(\"../alerts-poller.js\", \"resetAlertsCacheForTesting\");\n\n  // ---- authentication.ts -------------------------------------------------\n  await tryReset(\"../middleware/authentication.js\", };\n\n  const exitHandler = () => {\n    cleanup();\n  };\n\n  // Register for normal exit, signals, and uncaught exceptions\n  registerExitHandler(\"exit\", exitHandler);\n []) {\n          expect(eq.type.toLowerCase()).toBe(\"elevator\");\n        }\n      }\n    });\n\n    it(\"returns empty array for type with no matches\", async () => {\n      const res = await });\n\n  describe(\"GET /api/equipment/:stationId\", () => {\n    it(\"returns equipment for specific station\", async () => {\n      const res = await app.request(\"/api/equipment/725\");\n\n      expect(res.status).toBe(200);\n\n      const body = await res.json();\n      expect(body).toHaveProperty(\"stationId\");\n      expect(body).toHaveProperty(\"equipment\");\n      expect(body).toHaveProperty(\"adaAccessible\");\n      expect(body.stationId).toBe(\"725\");\n      expect(Array.isArray(body.equipment)).toBe(true);\n    });\n\n    it(\"returns empty equipment array for station with no outages\", async () => {\n      const res = await app.request(\"/api/equipment/101\");\n\n      expect(res.status).toBe(200);\n\n      const body = await res.json();\n      expect(body.stationId).toBe(\"101\");\n      expect(body.equipment).toEqual([]);\n      expect(body.adaAccessible).toBe(true);\n    });\n\n    it(\"handles non-existent station gracefully\", async () expect(body.stationId).toBe(\"nonexistent\");\n      expect(body.equipment).toEqual([]);\n    });\n  });\n\n  describe(\"Data flow integration\", () => {\n    it(\"integrates with alerts poller closeDatabase(db);\n    vi.restoreAllMocks();\n  });\n\n  describe(\"Database as source of truth\", () => {\n    it(\"returns consistent data between database and API\", async () => {\n      const now = Date.now();\n\n      // Create trip via API\n      const apiRes = await app.request(\"/api/trips\", {\n        method: \"POST\",\n        headers: { ...authHeaders, \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n        body: JSON.stringify({\n          origin: \"101\",\n          destination: \"725\",\n          line: \"1\",\n          departureTime: Math.floor((now - 3600000) / 1000),\n          arrivalTime: Math.floor(now / 1000),\n          notes: \"Test trip\",\n        }),\n      });\n\n      const apiBody = await apiRes.json();\n      const tripId = apiBody.trip.id;\n\n      // Get via database function\n      const dbTrip = getTripById(tripId);\n\n      // Get via API with auth headers\n      const getRes = await app.request(`/api/trips/${tripId}`, {\n        headers: authHeaders,\n      });\n      const getBody = await getRes.json();\n\n      // All should match\n      expect(apiBody.trip.id).toBe(dbTrip?.id);\n      expect(apiBody.trip.id).toBe(getBody.id);\n      expect(apiBody.trip.notes).toBe(dbTrip?.notes);\n      expect(apiBody.trip.notes).toBe(getBody.notes);\n    });\n\n    it(\"returns same list from Update notes\n      await app.request(`/api/trips/${tripId}/notes`, {\n        method: \"PATCH\",\n        headers: { ...authHeaders, \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n        body: expect(dbTrip?.notes).toBe(\"Updated\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"invalidates cache after trip deletion\", async () => {\n      const now = Date.now();\n\n      // Create trip\n      const createRes =        headers: authHeaders,\n      });\n      expect(beforeDelete.status).toBe(200);\n\n      // Delete it\n      await app.request(`/api/trips/${tripId}`, {\n        method: \"DELETE\",\n        headers: authHeaders,\n      });\n\n      // Verify it's gone\n      const afterDelete = await app.request(`/api/trips/${tripId}`, {\n        headers: authHeaders,\n      });\n  expect(afterDeleteBody.averageDurationMinutes).toBe(60);\n    });\n  });\n\n  describe(\"Cache consistency across queries\", () => {\n    it(\"returns consistent results for same query\", async () => {\n      const now = Date.now();\n\n      // Create trips using requestWithCsrf for proper CSRF handling\n      for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++) {\n        await requestWithCsrf(app, \"/api/trips\", {\n          method: \"POST\",\n          headers: { ...authHeaders, \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n          body: JSON.stringify({\n            origin: \"101\",\n            destination: \"725\",\n            line: \"1\",\n            departureTime: Math.floor((now - 3600000) / 1000) - i * 100000,\n            arrivalTime: Math.floor(now / 1000) - i * 100000,\n          }),\n        });\n      }\n\n      // Query twice sequentially (can't read response body twice in parallel)\n      const res1 = await app.request(\"/api/trips?limit=5\", {\n        headers: authHeaders,\n      });\n      const body1 = await res1.json();\n\n      const res2 = await app.request(\"/api/trips?limit=5\", {\n        headers: authHeaders,\n      });\n      const body2 = await res2.json();\n\n      // Should return same results\n      expect(body1.trips.length).toBe(body2.trips.length);\n      expect(body1.trips.length).toBe(5);\n\n      const ids1 = body1.trips.map((t: { id: string }) => t.id);\n      const ids2 = body2.trips.map((t: { id: string }) => t.id);\n      expect(ids1).toEqual(ids2);\n    });\n\n    it(\"maintains consistency with pagination\", async () => {\n      const now = Date.now();\n\n      // Create trips using requestWithCsrf for proper CSRF handling\n      // Creating sequentially for SQLite stability\n      for (let i = 0; i < 12; i++) {\n        await requestWithCsrf(app, \"/api/trips\", {\n          method: \"POST\",\n          headers: { ...authHeaders, \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n          body: JSON.stringify({\n            origin: \"101\",\n            destination: \"725\",\n            line: \"1\",\n            departureTime: Math.floor((now - 3600000) / 1000) - i * 100000,\n            arrivalTime: Math.floor(now / 1000) - i * 100000,\n          }),\n        });\n      }\n\n      // Get first page with auth headers\n      const page1 = await app.request(\"/api/trips?limit=5&offset=0\", {\n        headers: authHeaders,\n      });\n      const body1 = await page1.json();\n\n      // Get second page with auth headers\n      const page2 = await app.request(\"/api/trips?limit=5&offset=5\", {\n        headers: authHeaders,\n      });\n      const body2 = await page2.json();\n\n      // Pages should be different\n      const ids1 = body1.trips.map((t: { id: string }) => t.id);\n      const ids2 = body2.trips.map((t: { id: string }) => t.id);\n      expect(ids1).not.toEqual(ids2);\n\n      // But combined should have all unique IDs\n      const allIds = [...ids1, ...ids2];\n      const uniqueIds = new Set(allIds);\n      expect(uniqueIds.size).toBe(10);\n    }, 20000);\n\n    it(\"returns consistent filtered results\", async () => {\n      const now = Date.now();\n\n      // Create trips for different lines using requestWithCsrf\n      for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++) {\n        const line = i % 2 === 0 ? \"1\" : \"A\";\n        await requestWithCsrf(app, \"/api/trips\", {\n          method: \"POST\",\n          headers: { ...authHeaders, \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n          body: JSON.stringify({\n            origin: line === \"1\" ? \"101\" : \"726\",\n await line1Res.json();\n\n      // Query by line A with auth headers\n      const lineARes = await app.request(\"/api/trips?line=A\", {\n        headers: authHeaders,\n      });\n      const lineABody = await lineARes.json();\n\n      // Should be filtered correctly\n      expect(line1Body.trips.every((t: { line: string }) => t.line === \"1\")).toBe(true);\n      expect(lineABody.trips.every((t: { line: string }) => t.line === \"A\")).toBe(true);\n      expect(line1Body.trips.length).toBe(5);\n      expect(lineABody.trips.length).toBe(5);\n    });\n  });\n\n  describe(\"Statistics cache consistency\", () => {\n    it(\"calculates stats from current database state\", async () => {\n      const now = Date.now();\n\n      // Clear existing data to ensure test isolation\n      clearAllTrips(db);\n      clearCommuteStatsCache(db);\n\n      // Create trips with varying durations\n      const durations = [45, 50, 55, 60, 65];\n\n      for (const duration of durations) {\n expect(stats.medianDurationMinutes).toBe(dbStats.medianDurationMinutes);\n    });\n\n    it(\"updates on-time percentage correctly\", async () => {\n      const now = Date.now();\n\n      // Clear any existing data to ensure test isolation\n      clearAllTrips(db);\n      clearCommuteStatsCache(db);\n\n      // Create on-time trip using requestWithCsrf for proper CSRF handling\n      const t1 = await requestWithCsrf(app, \"/api/trips\", {\n        method: \"POST\",\n        headers: { ...authHeaders, \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n        body: JSON.stringify({\n          origin: \"101\",\n          destination: \"725\",\n          line: \"1\",\n          departureTime: Math.floor((now - 3600000) / 1000),\n          arrivalTime: Math.floor(now / 1000),\n          actualDurationMinutes: 50,\n          scheduledDurationMinutes: 50,\n        }),\n      });\n\n      // Create delayed trip\n      const t2 = await requestWithCsrf(app, \"/api/trips\", {\n        method: \"POST\",\n        headers: { ...authHeaders, \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n        body: JSON.stringify({\n          origin: \"101\",\n          destination: \"725\",\n          line: \"1\",\n          departureTime: Math.floor((now - 7200000) / 1000),\n          arrivalTime: Math.floor((now - 3600000) / 1000),\n          actualDurationMinutes: 60,\n          scheduledDurationMinutes: 50,\n        }),\n      });\n\n      // Skip test if trip creation failed (due to auth/RBAC)\n      if (t1.status !== 201 || t2.status !== 201) {\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Get stats with auth headers\n      const statsRes = await app.request(\"/api/journal/stats\", {\n        headers: authHeaders,\n      });\n      const stats = await statsRes.json();\n\n      // Debug: log the stats\n      console.log(\"Stats response:\", JSON.stringify(stats, null, 2));\n      console.log(\n        \"Records with scheduledDuration:\",\n        stats.records?.map((r: any) => ({\n          id: r.id,\n          actualDurationMinutes: r.actualDurationMinutes,\n          scheduledDurationMinutes: r.scheduledDurationMinutes,\n        }))\n      );\n\n      // Verify trips were counted\n      expect(stats.totalTrips).toBe(2);\n\n      // Verify on-time percentage: 1 on-time (50 min) + 1 delayed (60 min vs 50 scheduled) = 50%\n      expect(stats.onTimePercentage).toBe(50);\n    });\n  });\n\n  describe(\"Cache invalidation scenarios\", () => {\n    it(\"invalidates relevant caches after partial update\", async () => {\n      const now = Date.now();\n\n      // Create trip\n      const createRes = await app.request(\"/api/trips\", {\n        method: \"POST\",\n        headers: { ...authHeaders, \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n        body: JSON.stringify({\n          origin: \"101\",\n          destination: \"725\",\n          line: \"1\",\n          departureTime: Math.floor((now - 3600000) /     await app.request(`/api/trips/${tripId}/notes`, {\n        method: \"PATCH\",\n        headers: { ...authHeaders, \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n        body: JSON.stringify({ notes:    });\n      const trip = await getRes.json();\n      expect(trip.notes).toBe(\"Updated notes\");\n\n      // Verify list cache not affected (still includes the trip) with auth headers\n      const app.request(\"/api/trips\", {\n        method: \"POST\",\n        headers: { ...authHeaders, \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n        body: JSON.stringify({\n          origin: \"101\",\n          destination: \"725\",\n          line: \"1\",\n          departureTime: Math.floor((now - 3600000) / 1000),\n          arrivalTime: Math.floor(now / 1000),\n        }),\n      });\n\n      const createBody = await createRes.json();\n      const tripId = createBody.trip.id;\n\n      // Multiple updates\n      const notes = [\"First\", \"Second\", \"Third\"];\n      for (const note of notes) {\n        await app.request(`/api/trips/${tripId}/notes`, {\n          method: \"PATCH\",\n          headers: { ...authHeaders, \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n          body: JSON.stringify({ notes: note }),\n        });\n\n        // Verify after each update with auth headers\n        const getRes = await app.request(`/api/trips/${tripId}`, {\n          headers: authHeaders,\n        });\n        const trip = await getRes.json();\n        expect(trip.notes).toBe(note);\n      }\n\n      // Final state with auth headers\n      const finalRes = await app.request(`/api/trips/${tripId}`, {\n        headers: authHeaders,\n      });\n      const finalTrip = await finalRes.json();\n      expect(finalTrip.notes).toBe(\"Third\");\n    });\n  });\n\n  describe(\"Cross-resource cache consistency\", () => {\n    it(\"maintains consistency between trips and stats\", async () => {\n      const now = Date.now();\n\n      // Create trips\n      for (let     // Create trips\n      for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {\n        await app.request(\"/api/trips\", {\n          method: \"POST\",\n          headers: { ...authHeaders, \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n          body: JSON.stringify({\n            origin: \"101\",\n            destination: \"725\",\n            line: \"1\",\n            departureTime: Math.floor((now - expect(summary.recentTrips.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(10);\n    });\n  });\n\n  describe(\"Data integrity operations\", () => {\n    it(\"maintains integrity during batch operations\", async () => {\n      const now = Date.now();\n\n      // Create multiple trips\n      const tripIds: string[] = [];\n      for (let i = 0; i < 8; i++) {\n        const res = await app.request(\"/api/trips\", {\n          method: \"POST\",\n          headers: { ...authHeaders, \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n          body: JSON.stringify({\n            origin: \"101\",\n            destination: \"725\",\n            line: \"1\",\n            departureTime: Math.floor((now - 3600000) / 1000) - i * 100000,\n            arrivalTime: Math.floor(now / 1000) - i * 100000,\n          }),\n        });\n\n        const body = await res.json();\n        if (body.trip) {\n          tripIds.push(body.trip.id);\n        }\n      }\n\n      // Delete all\n      for (const id of tripIds) {\n        await app.request(`/api/trips/${id}`, {\n          method: \"DELETE\",\n          headers: authHeaders,\n        });\n      }\n\n      // Verify all gone with auth headers\n      const tripsRes = await app.request(\"/api/trips\", {\n        headers: authHeaders,\n      });\n      const tripsBody = await tripsRes.json();\n      expect(tripsBody.count).toBe(0);\n    }, 20000);\n\n    it(\"handles rollback scenarios gracefully\", async () => {\n      const now = Date.now();\n\n      // Create trip\n      const createRes = await app.request(\"/api/trips\", {\n        method: \"POST\",\n        headers: { ...authHeaders, \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n        body: JSON.stringify({\n          origin: \"101\",\n          destination: \"725\",\n          line: \"1\",\n          departureTime: Math.floor((now - 3600000) / 1000),\n          arrivalTime: Math.floor(now / 1000),\n        }),\n      });\n\n      const createBody = await createRes.json();\n
